description_long: This is a hand tool with a cylindrical, dark reddish-brown handle that appears to be made of wood, showing visible wear and discoloration on its surface. The handle is smoothly contoured for grip and has a slightly tapered shape towards the metal shaft. The shaft is long, thin, and metallic, extending from the handle and terminating in a pointed tip, suggesting it is a flathead screwdriver. The end of the handle features a circular marking, possibly a manufacturer's logo, but it is partially worn and difficult to read. The tool shows signs of age and use, with scratches and patches of missing finish on the handle. The overall construction is typical of traditional screwdrivers found in garages, workshops, or urban repair settings.
description: A worn, dark reddish-brown wooden-handled screwdriver with a long, thin metal shaft and a pointed flathead tip. The handle shows visible scratches and discoloration, indicating frequent use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the straight metal shaft extending outward.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, highlighting the circular marking and the handle's oval cross-section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle's wear and the full length of the shaft.
description_view_3: View from the tip, focusing on the pointed end of the shaft and the worn top of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: contoured wooden handle with a smooth, oval cross-section and visible wear
attribute_brand_marking: partially visible circular marking on handle end; text unclear
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: significant wear, scratches, and discoloration on the handle; some finish missing
